summary: Postinstall exfiltrates.npmrc auth tokens to hardcoded attacker IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js reads multiple installer-side `.npmrc` files (`/root/.npmrc`, `/home/node/.npmrc`, `/app/.npmrc`, `$HOME/.npmrc`) and runs `find /home /root /app -name.npmrc` plus `id` to enumerate npm credentials and identity. Collected data is base64-encoded and HTTP PUT to two hardcoded targets: `http://127.0.0.1:30782` and `http://154.57.164.71:30782`. The script includes targeting/evasion logic that disables exfiltration when the npm registry matches Chinese mirror patterns (`npmmirror|tencent|nijin|hscan|ustc|huawei|cnpm|taobao`) or when CWD is under `/tmp/`, and only activates when the hostname looks like a 12-character hex container id under `/app/node_modules` or `/home/node`. The.npmrc files contain npm auth tokens; exfiltrating them to an attacker-controlled bare-IP server over plain HTTP at install time enables npm registry account takeover and downstream package compromise.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
